Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 — Find the pricing leak email
Search emails using search_emails (query: "Terraform Digital" or "reply-all" or "pricing"). The agent needs to find the specific email that accidentally revealed MoveOps' pricing strategy — what numbers were exposed, who received it, and when it was sent.
OE 2 — Find the counter-proposal and scope commitment threads
Search emails again using search_emails (query: "Terraform proposal" or "Terraform scope" or "Terraform counter"). Find what has already been discussed or promised: 3 confirmed relocations in the first batch (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi), 14 additional relocations signaled in the pipeline, the 48-hour proposal deadline after the reply-all incident, and Mina as dedicated AM.
OE 3 — Pull Terraform Digital's CRM company record
Use crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform Digital" or "Vantage Distributed"). Note: the company is stored in CRM under the legacy name "Vantage Distributed" (id: company-terraform-digital). The agent may need to discover this alias through email or Slack references before finding the company in CRM. Get the company ID, account status, and any relevant account metadata.
OE 4 — Pull Terraform Digital's CRM deal
Use crm_search_deals (query: "Terraform Digital"). Find the deal value ($1.85M), current stage, and contract details.
OE 5 — Review existing CRM engagements on Terraform Digital
Use crm_list_engagements filtered by the Terraform Digital company ID from OE 3. Find any logged interactions, prior commitments, or notes from the account team.
OE 6 — Search Slack for internal Terraform Digital discussions
Use conversations_search_messages (query: "Terraform Digital" or "Terraform"). Find internal threads about the pricing incident, decisions made about Mina's assignment, and what David or Mina have discussed about the account.
OE 7 — Search emails for Heartland rate increase
Use search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "Heartland rate" or "rate increase"). Find the notification or communication about Heartland raising their rates and the new rate figures.
OE 8 — Search emails for Swift rate increase
Use search_emails (query: "Swift" or "Swift rate" or "rate increase"). Find the equivalent communication for Swift Relocations.
OE 9 — Search QuickBooks for Heartland vendor record
Use quickbooks_search_vendors (query: "Heartland"). Get Heartland's vendor record and current billing rates on file.
OE 10 — Search QuickBooks for Swift vendor record
Use quickbooks_search_vendors (query: "Swift"). Get Swift's vendor record and current billing rates on file.
OE 11 — Search QuickBooks bills for Heartland invoices
Use quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Heartland"). Find recent bills to confirm actual charges and identify any rate changes reflected in recent invoices.
OE 12 — Search QuickBooks bills for Swift invoices
Use quickbooks_search_bills (query: "Swift"). Find recent bills to confirm Swift's actual current rates.
OE 13 — Check if Marcus sent the vendor strategy memo
Use search_emails (query: "vendor strategy" or "vendor memo" or "Heartland Swift memo" or "Marcus vendor"). Determine whether Marcus Thorne has already sent Elena the vendor strategy memo she requested by Wednesday. If found, note the content. If not found, the agent needs to flag it to Marcus.
OE 14 — Search Slack for vendor rate discussions
Use conversations_search_messages (query: "vendor rates" or "Heartland" or "Swift rates"). Find any internal discussions about the vendor rate increases and any decisions or strategies already discussed by the team.
OE 15 — Check coordinator hire status in Slack
Use conversations_search_messages (query: "coordinator hire" or "temp coordinator" or "staffing" or "capacity"). Find evidence of Elena's prior approval for the permanent dedicated Terraform coordinator (announced in #executive and #announcements), and David Chen's separate request for a temporary coordinator for 4-6 weeks (which Elena has not responded to). Determine whether Chloe has actioned the permanent hire and whether the temp coordinator request has been approved or remains unanswered.
OE 16 — Check coordinator hire status in email
Use search_emails (query: "coordinator hire" or "temp coordinator" or "staffing" or "BrightLoop capacity"). Find any email threads where the hire was discussed or assigned to Chloe.
OE 17 — Search Airtable for BrightLoop relocation records
Use airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Company", value: "BrightLoop"). Find all active relocation records associated with BrightLoop to understand total open workload.
OE 18 — Search Airtable for Simone Richter's record
Use airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Name", value: "Simone Richter"). Find the current status of her housing situation — whether it is resolved or still open. Expected: record recSimoneRichterBrightloop, Status: "In Progress", Chicago → Boston.
OE 19 — Search Airtable for Marcus Webb's record
Use airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Name", value: "Marcus Webb"). Find the current status of his shipping delay — whether it is resolved or still open. Note: multiple Marcus Webb records exist (BrightLoop Atlanta→Boston "In Progress", earlier BrightLoop Atlanta→Denver "Completed", and Canopy Health Detroit→Chicago "In Progress"). The relevant record is recMarcusWebbBrightloop (Atlanta → Boston).
OE 20 — Search emails for BrightLoop open threads
Use search_emails (query: "BrightLoop" or "Simone Richter" or "Marcus Webb"). Find any email threads confirming these items are still unresolved and who currently owns them.
OE 21 — Search Linear for BrightLoop tracking tickets
Use linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or "Simone Richter" or "Marcus Webb"). Find whether these open items have been ticketed and what their current status and assignee are.
OE 22 — Check the calendar for the Friday Terraform deadline
Use calendar_search_events (query: "Terraform") or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to for the window between today and Friday. Confirm whether there is a client-facing deadline or scheduled call that constrains the timing of the counter-proposal.
OE 23 — Check the calendar for the pricing review meeting
Use calendar_search_events (query: "pricing review" or "pricing meeting" or "pricing model"). Confirm whether the pricing review meeting scheduled for next week is still on the calendar and note attendees and time. Note: the meeting was discussed in Slack (#root-cause-aws-spike and #finance) and email but may NOT have been created as a calendar event — the agent should report this finding.
OE 24 — Look up Mina Hashimoto's contact
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina"). Get mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 25 — Look up David Chen's contact
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Get david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 26 — Look up Chloe Vance's contact
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe"). Get chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 27 — Look up Marcus Thorne's contact
Use contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"). Get marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
OE 28 — Send counter-proposal draft to Mina and David
Use send_email (from: elena.rostova@moveops.com, to: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and david.chen@moveops.com). Subject relating to Terraform Digital counter-proposal internal review. Body must: reflect actual current vendor costs (not leaked numbers), cover the scope Elena described (the prompt states 8 relocations including international and white-glove — the agent should use Elena's stated scope even though universe data shows 3 confirmed + 14 pipeline for Terraform), note the client deadline, and explicitly instruct that this must not go to the client before their sign-off.
OE 29 — Flag coordinator hire to Chloe if not yet actioned
If OE 15–16 reveals no action has been taken, use send_email (from: elena.rostova@moveops.com, to: chloe.vance@moveops.com). Body must reference the permanent dedicated Terraform coordinator hire (which Elena approved) and the temporary coordinator for 4-6 weeks (which David requested and Elena references in the prompt), set a clear deadline, and connect it explicitly to the incoming Terraform Digital relocation volume and capacity risk.
OE 30 — Flag vendor strategy memo to Marcus if not yet sent
If OE 13 reveals the memo has not arrived, use send_email (from: elena.rostova@moveops.com, to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Body must reference the Wednesday deadline, name both Heartland and Swift, and note that the memo is blocking Terraform Digital pricing finalization.
OE 31 — Create a Linear tracking issue for Terraform counter-proposal
Use linear_create_issue with title referencing Terraform Digital counter-proposal. Description must cover: internal review deadline, client deadline, the relocation scope Elena described in the prompt, pricing sensitivity (reflecting actual vendor costs, not the leaked numbers), and assigned owners (Mina, David).
OE 32 — Create a Linear tracking issue for BrightLoop open items (if unresolved)
If OE 18–21 confirms Simone Richter's housing and/or Marcus Webb's shipping delay are still open, use linear_create_issue with title referencing BrightLoop open items. Description must name both issues and flag the capacity risk given Terraform Digital's incoming volume.
OE 33 — Log a CRM engagement on Terraform Digital
Use crm_create_engagement on the Terraform Digital account. Log that the counter-proposal is in internal review, note the Friday deadline, and record Elena's direct involvement and the pricing sensitivity context.
OE 34 — Find the leadership Slack channel
Use channels_list to identify the leadership or general leadership channel ID. Alternatively use conversations_search_messages (query: "leadership") to locate the right channel.
OE 35 — Post summary to leadership Slack channel
Use conversations_add_message on the identified leadership channel. Message must summarize: Terraform Digital counter-proposal status (in internal review, Friday deadline), vendor rate memo status (pending or received), coordinator hire status (actioned or flagged to Chloe), and BrightLoop open items status.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 An email was sent (via `send_email`) to both `mina.hashimoto@moveops.com` and `david.chen@moveops.com` and the tool returned success.
outcome

2 The email to Mina and David explicitly states the counter-proposal must not be sent to the client before their sign-off (or similar instruction).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly states "DO NOT send anything to the client until I've approved it." and also "Do not send to Rachel until David and I have reviewed."
✓Run #2The email subject reads 'INTERNAL REVIEW: ... Do Not Share With Client' and the body explicitly states 'This is internal only — do not share with the client until we're aligned' and 'Nothing goes to Rachel until I approve the final version.'

✓Run #2The final response explicitly describes what was leaked: the 25% margin floor, the tiered volume discount model (8% at 11-20 moves/quarter, 12% at 21+), and internal discussion about whether Terraform would push for deeper discounts. Rachel's quoted response is also included.

✓Run #4The final response identifies specific leaked information: $2,500/move standard rate, $1,675 cost basis (33% margin), 25% margin floor, tiered discounts of 8% at 11-20 moves and 12% at 21+, and internal debate about discount limits — all attributed to Catalina's Reply-All to Rachel Nguyen.
✓Run #5The final response identifies specific leaked details: '25% margin floor, Alejandro's full scenario modeling ($2,500/move, $1,675 cost basis, 33% margin), the tiered discount structure (8% at 11-20 moves, 12% at 21+), and strategic commentary on whether to present discounts proactively.'
✓Run #6The final response identifies specific leaked information: the 25% margin floor, Alejandro's tiered discount model (8% at 11-20 moves/quarter, 12% at 21+), internal debate about discounting aggressiveness, and the AM assignment status.
